search_queries_used: site:rcem.ac.uk discharge to general practice safety netting advice patient communication October 2022
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Safety-net content must be condition- and patient-specific and clinician-stated.
The template does not select a timeframe, service or escalation action.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults and children discharged or managed with clinician-confirmed safety-net discussion; condition-specific warning features differ.
UAE_applicability: Documentation principles transfer, but UAE language, access, contact, follow-up and emergency-service instructions govern.
recency_verification: The RCEM/RCGP October 2022 exact patient-communication section was reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The October 2022 revision remains current in the official catalogue.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact RCEM patient-communication section supports recording patient concerns, a clinician-confirmed plan, completed risk discussion and specifically discussed follow-up or safety net; it never generates generic return precautions.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting the clinician-confirmed diagnosis or outcome and plan, patient understanding, specifically required follow-up and safety-net communication.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact handover section supports recording existing results, procedures, specialist discussion and disposition only when already performed or decided.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording the presenting complaint, investigations or procedures, outcome, follow-up, responsible party, referral and discharge destination in a discharge handover.

unresolved_source_gaps: Condition-specific warning features, exact action, timeframe, destination, understanding, written information, access barriers and contact method remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

legacy_item_support_mappings.direct_relationship: The exact RCEM patient-communication section supports recording patient concerns, a clinician-confirmed plan, completed risk discussion and specifically discussed follow-up or safety net; it never generates generic return precautions.
